rules.requirement: Authenticated application work uses an approved target profile, shell, and layout archetype.
Global destinations are not duplicated across competing navigation owners.
Narrative content does not exceed an approximately 800px readable measure without an explicit decision.
A docked supporting pane transforms before primary reading width falls below approximately 640px.
Board columns preserve a useful 280px minimum and normally target approximately 320px.
A timeline preserves a semantic pane or documents an accessible alternative.
The primary task and viewport do not become accidental competing vertical scroll owners.
Deep workbench tools remain one application frame rather than a set of unrelated floating cards.
Focus mode restores prior layout, selection, open tools, and scroll.
Routine pointer-first desktop controls use approved 28px, 32px, or 36px tiers.
No declared action region has more than one dominant primary action.
Icon-only controls have an accessible name and non-hover access path.
Visible focus is not removed or replaced by an imperceptible treatment.
Effective targets and spacing meet the selected platform and input-mode minimums.
Links navigate, buttons issue commands, and selection controls expose current value.
Raw one-off colors are prohibited outside approved token definitions or exceptions.
State and meaning are not communicated by color alone.
Accent color does not dominate persistent navigation or broad page backgrounds.
Routine radii above 16px require a documented component or brand reason.
Nested paper shadows are prohibited.
Persistent rows and navigation items do not use external drop shadows.
Application text below 12px equivalent requires explicit accessibility review.
Aligned metrics and dates use tabular numerals where supported.
Essential instructions and status do not use muted contrast or ambiguous wording.
Equal-card dashboard grids require a documented recurring decision need.
Toolbar, body, and footer regions belonging to one object use one integrated outer surface.
Local tabs preserve object context and are not used for unrelated global destinations.
Deep navigation nesting requires an information-architecture review.
Every screen has explicit global, object, view, local, metadata, and temporary ownership where applicable.
Meaningful web location and view state are restorable or shareable when safe.
Meaningful repeated actions are keyboard operable and discoverable through an appropriate command surface.
Buttons, menus, palettes, context menus, and shortcuts invoke one permission-aware command implementation.
Loading, empty, error, permission, and unavailable states are implemented where applicable.
Local mutations do not force a full-page or full-shell reset without justification.
Drafts and valid input survive validation, network, permission, and conflict failure where feasible.
The applicable viewport/window and input matrix is captured and reviewed.
Constrained layouts change representation before labels, relationships, or primary content become unusable.
Keyboard path, semantics, labels, contrast, focus, zoom/scaling, and screen-reader behavior pass automated and manual checks.
Drag, gesture, hover, chart, and spatial interactions provide non-spatial or non-gesture alternatives.
Non-essential animation has reduced-motion behavior.
User-driven motion remains interruptible and begins from the presented value.
Primary actions use specific verb-first result labels.
Errors and destructive actions name the object, consequence, retained state, and recovery.
No completion, synchronization, publication, verification, collaboration, or deployment claim exceeds underlying evidence.
High-impact work exposes scope, environment, permissions, side effects, authority, and recovery before commitment.
Agent proposals, execution, evidence, acceptance, and deployment are represented as distinct states.
People can pause, redirect, narrow, revoke, review, or take over agent work without losing valid isolated progress.
Presence, notification, assignment, and shared-state claims correspond to durable system state.
Concurrent changes do not silently overwrite another participant’s work.
Primary interactions acknowledge input immediately and avoid avoidable blocking work.
Loading, media, fonts, data updates, and animation do not create destructive layout instability.
New UI uses an approved production component mapping or records a design-system gap.
Representative golden references and realistic fixtures exist for adopted shells and critical flows.
Project context, terminology, production mappings, and golden references are completed before claiming full design-system conformance.
Every durable route, native screen, window, major modal flow, and public page maps to an approved shell, layout archetype, page or flow, regions, states, and production implementation.
Every shipped theme and asset family has an executable production source, design mapping, target scope, accessibility behavior, and owner.
Completion evidence is tied to the exact source revision, target, environment, fixture, and time.
Intentional exceptions identify rule, reason, scope, owner, accessibility impact, and expiration or migration.
